recurso
Product Docs Pricing Compare
Star on GitHub Get started

Compare

Recurso vs Chargebee

Chargebee is a mature proprietary billing suite with deep integrations, CPQ, and polished dunning — and a platform fee that scales with your MRR. Recurso is open-source, self-hosted, and MIT-licensed: $0 in software fees, and the code is yours. You give up Chargebee's breadth; you keep your revenue and your data.

Start self-hosting Read the quickstart

MIT licensed · Go + PostgreSQL + TigerBeetle · Stripe & Razorpay

At a glance

Own the code vs buy the suite

Chargebee buys you breadth and a managed product. Recurso gives you ownership, self-hosting, and no revenue cut.

Dimension Recurso Chargebee
Model Open source (MIT), self-hosted Proprietary SaaS
Platform fee None$0 software fee; gateway fees still apply ~0.30–1.25% of MRROn top of gateway processing fees
Self-hosting / data residency Full — run it in your own VPC, your data never leaves Vendor-hosted; data lives with Chargebee
Source access & lock-in Full source, no lock-in — fork and own it Closed source; migration cost to leave
Integration breadth Focused set; extend via API and MIT source Very broad catalog of native integrations — a real strength
CPQ & sales-led billing Not a focus Mature CPQ and quote-to-cash tooling
Dunning Algorithmic retries (epsilon-greedy, Thompson, UCB1) with recovered-revenue attribution Mature, well-proven dunning and recovery tooling
India compliance GST e-invoicing (IRP), RBI e-mandates with 24-hour pre-debit notices, DPDP consent — built in Strong India + global tax support (Chargebee has deep India roots)
Accounting ledger Built-in double-entry ledger; optional TigerBeetle mirror Revenue & RevRec reporting; not a self-hosted ledger
Maturity New project, small community Mature, widely adopted, well-supported

Fee ranges reflect Chargebee's publicly described pricing model as of mid-2026 and vary by plan, volume, and contract. Confirm current terms directly with Chargebee.

Total cost of ownership

What a percentage of MRR adds up to

An illustrative sketch — not a quote. The point is that a platform fee is a tax that grows exactly as you do.

Annual recurring revenue Chargebee platform fee
at ~0.5% of MRR		 …at ~0.9% of MRR Recurso software fee
$1M ARR ~$5,000/yr ~$9,000/yr $0
$5M ARR ~$25,000/yr ~$45,000/yr $0
$20M ARR ~$100,000/yr ~$180,000/yr $0

Illustrative only. The platform fee is on top of payment-gateway processing fees, which you pay either way. Recurso's software fee is $0, but self-hosting has real costs of its own — infrastructure, and the engineering time to run and maintain it. The honest comparison is Chargebee's platform fee versus your hosting and ops cost; for most teams the crossover favors self-hosting as ARR grows, but not at every stage.

The honest verdict

Which one should you run?

Choose Chargebee if…

  • You want a mature, managed product and would rather not run infrastructure
  • You need its broad native-integration catalog and CPQ / quote-to-cash tooling
  • A percentage-of-MRR platform fee is an acceptable trade for that breadth and support
  • You don't need to self-host or keep billing data inside your own infrastructure

Choose Recurso if…

  • You don't want to pay a percentage of your revenue to a billing vendor
  • You need to self-host and keep billing data inside your own VPC
  • You want the source code, no lock-in, and the freedom to fork under MIT
  • You want a double-entry ledger and India-native GST / RBI / DPDP compliance built in
  • You have the engineering capacity to run your own billing stack
vs Lago vs Flexprice vs Kill Bill vs Stripe Billing

Stop paying a tax on your own revenue

Free forever on your own infrastructure. Clone the repo and you're minutes away from a seeded dashboard.

Start self-hosting Read the quickstart

git clone https://github.com/swapnull-in/recur-so.git && make demo

recurso

The open-source billing engine for SaaS. Built with Go, PostgreSQL, and TigerBeetle. MIT licensed.
